qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded gzip decompression in FITS image plugin enables memory-exhaustion denial of service
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is in Pillow's FitsImagePlugin.py and concerns unbounded gzip decompression causing memory exhaustion in FITS images. The advisory concerns an out-of-bounds write (CWE-787) in Pillow's PSD image loader. Different image format (FITS vs PSD), different source file (FitsImagePlugin.py vs PsdImagePlugin.py), different vulnerability type (memory exhaustion DoS vs out-of-bounds write), different root cause mechanism.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory describes an out-of-bounds write (CWE-787) triggered when loading crafted PSD images in Pillow's PsdImagePlugin. The sole qualifying finding (fits_gzip_memory_exhaustion) is in Pillow but targets FitsImagePlugin.py with a different vulnerability class (memory exhaustion DoS via gzip decompression), different image format (FITS vs PSD), and different root cause (missing decompressed-size limit vs out-of-bounds write in PSD pixel data handling).
evidence: Advisory references PR #9427 and commits 54ba4db and 9000313 in PsdImagePlugin.py related to PSD image loading.
The fits_gzip_memory_exhaustion finding targets FitsImagePlugin.py:131 (gzip.decompress) with a different root cause and impact.
No finding references PsdImagePlugin.py, PSD image handling, or out-of-bounds write conditions.
